title: SpaceX achieves multiple milestones: enters Nasdaq-100, its AI surpasses Google, and analysts set record price target
summary: Elon Musk's space company grabs media attention with its inclusion in the benchmark index, the superior performance of its artificial intelligence model, a new price target from analysts, and the challenges for its eventual IPO.

title: Analysts recommend Nvidia as one of the best quality stocks and the only long-term AI stock
summary: Several Wall Street reports highlight the chip maker as a solid investment in artificial intelligence, with key investor questions and buy recommendations.

title: AI memory chip market to reach $476 billion by 2030, with Micron and Western Digital as favorites
summary: Explosive growth in demand for memory chips driven by artificial intelligence is expected, with companies like Micron and Western Digital positioned to benefit.

title: Three AI infrastructure stocks could double in value by 2027, including Celestica and GCT Semiconductor
summary: Analysts identify opportunities in the AI infrastructure sector, highlighting companies like Celestica and semiconductor firm GCT.

title: Major energy company files for bankruptcy
summary: A leading energy company files for Chapter 11 amid financial difficulties, in a move that affects the energy market.

title: Another firearms retailer files for bankruptcy
summary: A financially troubled gun store chain joins the list of retailers filing for Chapter 11 bankruptcy.

title: AMC Entertainment sells 95.25 million shares to institutional investors for $200 million
summary: The movie theater chain conducts a private placement of shares to strengthen its capital and finance its operations.

title: Pizza chain to close up to 50 locations after years of decline
summary: A well-known pizza franchise announces the closure of up to 50 stores due to falling sales and changes in consumer habits.

title: Costco and Walmart crowned as kings of supermarkets in the United States
summary: The retail giants lead grocery sales, surpassing traditional chains in a changing market.

title: Mark Cuban criticizes AI companies for job losses
summary: Billionaire investor Mark Cuban warns that artificial intelligence companies are not taking responsibility for job destruction.

title: Sam Altman expects OpenAI to reach a trillion-dollar valuation as SoftBank faces a $40 billion loan
summary: OpenAI's CEO seeks an astronomical valuation for his company, while its main investor, SoftBank, faces a multimillion-dollar debt maturity in 2027.

title: Republican Senator Phil Gramm, Trump ally, dies at 71
summary: Former US Senator Phil Gramm, known for his hardline stance against Iran and his closeness to Donald Trump, died from a sudden illness, generating international reactions.

title: Israel to hold general elections on October 27 in a plebiscite on Netanyahu
summary: The Israeli government calls early elections to decide the future of Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu amid the political crisis.

title: FIFA president proposes expanding 2030 World Cup to 64 teams
summary: Gianni Infantino raises the possibility of expanding the 2030 World Cup to 64 teams, as a topic of debate for the future of the tournament.

title: Zelensky announces replacement of Ukraine's prime minister to regain political support
summary: President Volodymyr Zelensky seeks to revitalize his government with a new prime minister amid the conflict with Russia.

title: Ford and Canadian union Unifor reach tentative labor agreement
summary: Ford Motor Company and Canada's Unifor union reach a tentative contract agreement that could avoid a strike.

title: China tightens regulations on corporate bond credit ratings
summary: The Chinese government strengthens control over rating agencies to prevent inflated ratings in the corporate bond market.

title: Large private equity firms raise more funds in a winner-takes-all market
summary: Leading private equity firms manage to attract capital even in a competitive environment, consolidating their dominant position.

title: Low valuations of Korean stocks seen as opportunity amid AI boom
summary: Investors find valuations of the South Korean stock market attractive, trading at low levels despite the artificial intelligence earnings boom.

title: Wall Street is optimistic about one of these chip stocks and pessimistic about the other
summary: A Wall Street analysis reveals a divergence of opinions between two major semiconductor manufacturers, pointing to one as a winner and the other as a loser.
